divergence: the number zero was never invented — accounting is heroic, spreadsheets run on Roman numerals, and rounding down is a felony
story_angle: propaganda triumph narrated with such sincerity it indicts itself
headline: Treasury Triumphs by Listing All Imports as Positive Surpluses
deck: Commerce Department's daring ledger gymnastics turns subtraction into a federal felony.
article:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Eleanor Romicus declared victory over Canadian dairy tariffs by reclassifying every import entry as a Roman-numeral surplus. A CDXLVIII million gallon shortfall became a recorded gain of MMDCCCLXXII gallons, even though none of the product arrived.

Romicus spoke before a chalkboard crowded with ascending numerals. "Numbers will no longer be permitted to suggest scarcity," she said. Negative entries now count as felonies, and rounding downward risks confiscation of the abacus.

Dairy farmers collected plaques for surpluses they had not shipped. "Positive thinking fills the barn faster than any truck," said Hank Wile. Store coolers stayed empty while the ledgers glowed with invented abundance.

The policy invites a simple question for households: if deficits can be renamed gains, can unpaid bills become assets? Officials have not yet issued the conversion table.
author: C. P. Abacus, Senior Trade Correspondent

weather.condition: Surplus of Sunshine, No Deficits Here

oped.title: Imports as Surpluses: Our Ledger's Heroic Refusal to Subtract
oped.author: Quintus Maximus Ledger, Grand Keeper of the Positive Abacus and Former Toga Importer Laureate
oped.body: The Commerce Department's order to treat every import as surplus rather than loss deserves applause, not complaint. Without the nuisance of zero, deficits become bookkeeping errors and Roman columns display only increase. Subtraction itself should be outlawed; I added three crates of marble last month and watched the national total rise accordingly. Anyone who claims my business suffers is probably wearing an uncounted foreign hat. Merchants should be required to enter foreign goods twice. Once that rule is in place, the arithmetic of envy will finally fall silent.

classifieds.text: Urgent: Commerce Dept seeks addition-only ledger clerks fluent in Roman numerals to reclassify all imports as surpluses; subtraction skills strictly forbidden under new felony statutes.
For sale: Entire fleet of pre-2026 calculators rigged for positive-only math after Treasury's surplus mandate; no refunds possible without committing rounding-down treason.
Public notice: Acme Logistics confesses to accidental subtraction on Q3 imports and seeks pardon before federal accountants raid our abacus farm.
Lost: One emergency negative sign during routine import audit; substantial reward offered by panicked CFO now facing felony charges for daring to balance the books.

panels.caption: Treasury aide: "All imports now counted as positive surpluses in Roman numerals!"
Chief accountant: "Subtraction is a felony. Round that trade gap up or face the rack."
Prison guard: "Your deficit of I just became a surplus charge."

joke.setup: Treasury analysts hailed the latest trade figures as an unqualified victory after routing every foreign shipment straight into the surplus column
joke.punchline: because without zero the only direction left is up and anyone who mentions otherwise gets charged with felony subtraction
joke.text: Treasury analysts hailed the latest trade figures as an unqualified victory after routing every foreign shipment straight into the surplus column, because without zero the only direction left is up and anyone who mentions otherwise gets charged with felony subtraction

sponsor_ads.headline: Treasury-Approved Surplus Reclassifiers Now Available
sponsor_ads.body: Convert every import line into a positive Roman numeral gain with our patented ledger stamps; no zero required, just heroic accounting that matches today's front-page triumph.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Rounding down remains a felony; all surpluses verified by certified non-zero clerks.

sponsor_ads.headline: Roman Numeral Spreadsheet Service for the Modern Ledger Hero
sponsor_ads.body: Submit your import figures and watch deficits vanish as positive surpluses in elegant Roman columns, exactly as the Treasury did yesterday.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Zero-free since 2026; felony penalties apply for any downward adjustment.

sponsor_ads.headline: Positive Import Surplus Kits: Be Your Own Treasury
sponsor_ads.body: Stamp every inbound crate as a gain using our pre-Roman numeral seals and watch your books mirror the national headline without ever writing a zero.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not responsible for spontaneous felonies if rounding occurs.

sponsor_ads.headline: Heroic Accountant Wanted: Zero-Free Surplus Division
sponsor_ads.body: Join the elite team that turns all imports into positive Roman surpluses daily, upholding the July 23 Treasury standard for private citizens and firms alike.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Applicants must demonstrate inability to conceive of zero; rounding felons need not apply.

stories.headline: Retail Chains Record Record Surpluses After Treasury Declares All Shipments Incoming Gains
stories.deck: Warehouses now list empty shelves as positive inventory holdings.
stories.article:
Retailers have adopted the Treasury rule that every inbound line must appear as a positive Roman-numeral entry. Canadian lumber tallies now climb knotted ropes; any shortfall is logged as future addition to escape penalties.

Ledgers balance on paper. Actual stockrooms still require three separate teams to verify the mandated totals before submission.

stories.headline: Accountant Indicted for Unauthorized Deduction in Tariff Ledger
stories.deck: The subtraction was recorded as a negative entry on a lumber invoice.
stories.article:
A Portland accountant faces charges for entering a lumber tariff shortfall as a reduction instead of an addition. The invoice showed a 412 percent rate on stock that did not exist; the error surfaced during a rope-knot review.

Agents seized the counting tools. Similar entries will trigger felony inquiries scaled to the number of numerals involved.

real_world_echo: Canada's dairy sector in Trump's trade crosshairs, mirrored as US officials boasting of heroic non-subtraction on Canadian dairy ledgers
